Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets File Process Scheduled Task Malware Artifact Target System Artifact Open Source Intelligence (OSINT) User Account OS Process File System Network Traffic Network Share Domain Name Registrar
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method File Metadata (Mechanism) Reg.exe Utility Process Creation (Mechanism) Command Execution (Mechanism) Scheduled Task/Job Bootkit Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism Network Topology User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Process Discovery System Network Configuration Discovery User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) Network Share Access (Mechanism) Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) Remote Desktop Protocol

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies potential targets by utilizing a resource leak on the target to deplete the quantity of the resource available to service legitimate requests.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker maps network topology and identifies vulnerabilities by monitoring network traffic between nodes of a public or multicast network in an attempt to capture sensitive information at the protocol level.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker creates custom malware for water and wastewater systems exploitation by altering tools used in a development environment.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker targets specific employees with tailored emails to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerable web applications to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-663: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: The attacker runs malicious code on compromised systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker maintains access through the boot process.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker gains higher-level access to systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker avoids detection and attribution by mapping network topology.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker gathers credentials for further access.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker identifies system information.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker maps network configuration.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally using valid accounts.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker uses remote services for movement.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects sensitive data.

stage.name: Collection
stage.technique_name: T1039: Data from Network Shared Drive
stage.capec: CAPEC-643: Identify Shared Files/Directories on System
stage.description: The attacker monitors network traffic.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates stolen data.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker transfers files securely.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker destroys sensitive data.

Scenario.summary: The attacker conducts a complex attack on water and wastewater systems, starting with reconnaissance and resource development, then gaining initial access, executing malicious code, maintaining persistence, escalating privileges, evading defense mechanisms, accessing credentials, discovering system information, moving laterally, collecting sensitive data, exfiltrating stolen data, and finally disrupting critical systems and destroying sensitive data.
